title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again; rates up by 90 paise per litre
content: Fuel prices have seen another surge, with petrol and diesel rates climbing by approximately 90 paise per litre on Tuesday. This marks the second increase in less than a week, pushing petrol prices in Delhi to Rs 98.64 and diesel to Rs 91.58.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: With 7 matches to go, SRH and GT qualify; final spot up for grabs
content: With seven games left, Royal Challengers Bangalore, Gujarat Titans, and Sunrisers Hyderabad have secured playoff spots. Lucknow Super Giants and Mumbai Indians are out. Rajasthan Royals are in a strong position. Punjab Kings, Kolkata Knight Riders, Delhi Capitals, and Chennai Super Kings have slim hopes. The race for the remaining spots is intense with many possible outcomes.

title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei makes a 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: Elon Musk loses $150 billion OpenAI lawsuit; jury says he sued too late
content: Elon Musk has lost his $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI. A nine-person Oakland jury has unanimously cleared OpenAI, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman and Microsoft in Elon Musk's $150 billion lawsuit, ruling the Tesla CEO sued too late. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers dismissed the case on the spot. Musk had accused them of "stealing a charity" he co-founded in 2015. The verdict clears OpenAI's path to a planned $1 trillion IPO.

title: Ebola virus outbreak: American doctor infected while treating patients in Congo
content: A rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain has infected an American doctor in the Democratic Republic of Congo, prompting the WHO to declare a public health emergency. The outbreak has spread to Uganda, with health experts citing delayed response due to initial misidentification of the virus. The US is enhancing screening and entry restrictions to prevent further spread.

title: Salesforce CEO who halted hiring sees company spending millions on Anthropic tokens
content: Salesforce is significantly boosting AI spending, projecting hundreds of millions on Anthropic tokens, while halting software engineer hiring. CEO Marc Benioff highlights AI's role in enhancing engineer productivity by over 30%, enabling faster output and lower development costs. This strategic shift reflects a broader tech trend of leveraging AI for efficiency, with humans still integral to operations.

title: At 43.4°C, Delhi records hottest day of season; IMD predicts temperature may hit 45°C
content: Delhi experienced its highest maximum temperature of the season on Monday, with several areas exceeding 44°C. The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions, forecasting temperatures to reach 45°C by Friday. Maximum temperatures are expected to rise by approximately 2°C over the next two days.

title: US Immigration authority: Starting July 10, you will be denied visa for this
content: USCIS now has the power to reject or deny immigration applications, even keeping fees, if an invalid signature is found months or years later. This new rule, effective July 10, 2026, applies broadly to most benefit requests, including visas and Green Cards. Applicants cannot usually fix signature errors after submission.

title: Twisha death case: CCTV shows her heading to terrace; husband, others attempt CPR later
content: A chilling CCTV video shows Twisha Sharma ascending to her terrace shortly before her death in Bhopal.  Her husband, Samarth Singh, is absconding, with a reward offered.  The case has taken a turn as the mother-in-law, a retired judge, claims Twisha had psychiatric issues and substance abuse problems, contradicting dowry harassment allegations.

title: Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf net worth 2026: Earnings and investments
content: Steffi Graf and Andre Agassi are a very recognizable tennis pair, and they have an estimated net worth in 2026 of $290 million. They’ve become wealthy by winning Grand Slams, signing multiyear contracts and other investments. Both benefited from the brand equity, business pursuits and financial planning that they developed during and after their successful professional tennis careers.

title: FIFA 2026 controversy erupts as fans slam BTS World Cup song credits
content: Fans are criticizing BigHit Music after BTS was reportedly credited on Jungkook’s solo FIFA World Cup song Dreamers. Many argued that Jungkook alone created and performed the track, calling the move unfair to his solo achievements. The backlash grew amid fresh buzz about BTS potentially performing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show, reigniting conversations about solo recognition, group branding, and BigHit’s marketing strategy surrounding Jungkook’s global success.

title: Aaron Rodgers not spotted with wife despite Steelers buzz and rumors
content: Aaron Rodgers, the NFL veteran, recently signed a one year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers, as retirement rumors dominated the headlines, sparking chaos among millions of fans. Amid all the speculations, the spotlight remains on Aaron Rodgers’ wife even as he continues to maintain privacy about his marital life. Through all of this, a picture of Aaron Rodgers with a woman has convinced millions that the identity of his wife has finally been revealed.

title: Oil above $100: How soaring crude prices are hitting India — explained in 10 charts
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are significantly impacting India's economy. Rising crude oil prices have led to a Rs 3 per litre fuel hike, eroded investor wealth by over Rs 5.85 lakh crore, and pushed the rupee to an all-time low. Foreign investors are also withdrawing funds, while the import bill has surged.

title: Key accused in Bengal CM Suvendu aide's murder arrested in UP's Muzaffarnagar
content: On May 11, a Special Investigation Team (SIT) probing Rath's murder had arrested three people before widening its probe across multiple states. The cops suspected that hired sharpshooters were brought in from outside West Bengal to carry out the killing with military-style precision. A key breakthrough came after investigators traced a UPI payment made at the Nivedita Setu toll plaza on May 6.

title: Tempers flare as Samson and Klaasen clash after stumping dismissal - Watch
content: The incident took place on the third ball of the 15th over of SRH’s chase when Noor Ahmad dismissed Klaasen with a stumping.

title: Mike Vrabel battles marriage rumors as Dianna Russini affair buzz grows
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach’s alleged involvement with the former New York Times’ NFL insider and reporter, Dianna Russini, has sparked shockwaves across the league as millions were angry about it. While Dianna Russini seems to have moved on from the alleged affair drama as she was recently spotted having dinner with her friends and husband, it seems Mike Vrabel is still trying to get over the drama.

title: Tom Kane, voice of Yoda and Professor Utonium, dies at 64
content: Tom Kane, best known for voicing Professor Utonium in the beloved animated series The Powerpuff Girls, has passed away at the age of 64. The veteran voice actor’s death was confirmed by TMZ through his representative. According to his representative, Zach McGinnis, Tom Kane died due to complications arising from the stroke he suffered in 2020. The actor had reportedly been hospitalised in Kansas City and passed away at the hospital.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad: CSK's 'Arjuna' who refuses to attack
content: Among all batters who have faced at least 100 balls in the powerplay this season, Gaikwad has the lowest strike rate — 124.6. For comparison, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has scored at 244.51 and Abhishek Sharma at 228.87.

title: William Davis dies at 22, months after Virginia Union transfer
content: William Davis, a 22-year-old defensive back at Sam Houston State University, has died unexpectedly, according to official statements shared with TMZ Sports. Davis recently transferred from Virginia Union University and had joined the Bearkats for spring football workouts. Head coach Phil Longo remembered him as a positive and passionate teammate. The university plans to honor Davis during the 2026 football season.

title: Rare! Gukesh's challenger Sindarov plays against bedridden Firouzja - Watch
content: Alireza Firouzja stunned the chess world by playing his Round 5 classical game against Javokhir Sindarov from a hotel room bed at the Superbet Chess Classic Romania 2026 after suffering an ankle injury. The extraordinary setup quickly went viral. Earlier, R Praggnanandhaa defeated Sindarov in a significant round-two encounter in Bucharest.

title: Bengaluru: Rains again throws normal life out of gear
content: The maximum temperature at Bengaluru city was 34℃, at HAL Airport and KIA, the maximum temperature was 34.4℃ and 35℃ respectively

title: KSRTC warns employees against joining indefinite strike from May 20
content: According to the circular, employees working in transport corporations are barred from resorting to strikes under the provisions of the Act

title: U.S. Justice Department drops all charges against Gautam Adani; case closed permanently
content: The case turned in Adani’s favour after prosecutors found no clear U.S. linkages and insufficient evidence to sustain the allegations, according to people familiar with the matter

title: Karnataka may face fertilizer shortage during kharif season: Cheluvarayaswamy
content: He said that the State had been bracing for a potential shortage of fertilizers in the 2026 kharif season, as supplies were being adversely affected ahead of the monsoon

title: Church bodies offer to broker Kuki-Naga peace in Manipur
content: Leaders of two church organisations meet Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh in a bid to end the conflict between the two communities in the State

title: Maharashtra Congress to hold training camp for 7,000 office-bearers
content: The training programme is intended to familiarise new functionaries with party organisation, election procedures, and administrative responsibilities

title: Bombay High Court quashes Maharashtra GRs on school disqualification
content: The Bombay High Court, at its Kolhapur bench, on April 29 ordered that two government resolutions (GRs) issued on April 1 and April 2 are not applicable to the petitioner schools

title: NTK will go it alone in the next Lok Sabha elections: Seeman
content: Seeman urged his party cadre not to get disheartened by the recent Assembly election results, which saw the party’s vote share drop by 50

title: Dindoshi sessions court denies anticipatory bail to grandfather in child assault case
content: The court said the accused may flee or tamper with evidence, and placed the welfare of the child victim above other considerations

title: Olympian Jennifer Paes, Leander's mother passes away
content: Jennifer Paes was the great-great-granddaughter of noted Bengali poet and dramatist Michael Madhusudan Dutt

title: West Bengal Police reopens 59 cases linked to 2021 post-poll violence, registers 181 fresh FIRs
content: Multiple Trinamool leaders come under the police crackdown as many face arrests in corruption and post-poll violence charges

title: Who is Abhijeet Dipke? Inside the viral Cockroach Janta Party movement
content: What began as a satirical response to Chief Justice of India Surya Kant’s oral observation has grown into an online movement reflecting youth anger, political satire, and distrust in institutions

title: CAA applicants must declare passport status, surrender foreign passports: Home Ministry
content: Home Ministry notifies amendment to Citizenship Rules; move comes days after BJP came to power for the first time in West Bengal where a large number of Matua community, the intended beneficiaries of CAA legislation reside

title: Address ‘ground level challenges’, ease regulatory frameworks: Norwegian businesses tell Modi
content: At India-Norway Business and Research Summit, Modi, Store discuss enhancing investment, trade in India

title: India is now free from Maoism, says Amit Shah at Bastar
content: Home Minister says the country is largely free from security challenges of Jammu & Kashmir, the Northeast, and Naxalism; he urges the people of Bastar to “accept” about 3,000 surrendered Naxals and help them become part of the mainstream

title: Parliamentary Standing Committee to review the reforms of National Testing Agency
content: The agenda includes a review of the implementation of the K. Radhakrishnan Committee report on NTA reforms, as well as an update on the investigation into the alleged NEET-UG paper leak case

title: Haryana Cabinet clears industrial policy, rules to curb vehicular pollution
content: The policy aims to draw fresh investments worth ₹5 lakh crore, create 10 lakh jobs, and boost the State’s export figures over the next five years

title: Karnataka-Kerala connection: P.C. Vishnunadh’s induction into V.D. Satheesan cabinet brings cheer to people of Tumari in Sagar
content: P.C. Vishnunadh, who took oath as a Minister in the newly sworn-in Kerala government, is married to Ha.Ma. Kanaka, a Kannada poet and native of Haalkere village in Tumari Gram Panchayat

title: Focus shifts to tussle within  AIADMK ahead of district  secretaries’ meeting with EPS
content: A cross-section of the party functionaries feels that Mr.  Palaniswami should follow up on his statement calling on the dissidents to agree to talks with an appeal for reunification

title: FIR against government officials for not doing Census work
content: A complaint by a Census official alleges the employees concerned defied government orders by not collecting data and not making a single entry in the designated house listing operation (HLO) mobile app, despite repeated warnings to begin their work

title: 38 million full-time jobs may fall if war in West Asia continues, warns ILO
content: The report warned that if fuel or fertilizer prices rise, or shortages persist, the effects could extend beyond farm incomes to food prices, rural livelihoods and food security, in countries that depend on imported fertilizers, including India

title: Development works worth ₹1 crore sanctioned for water conservation: MLA
content: Launching tank development works in Orvakal mandal, Ms. Charitha Reddy says the government is taking various measures to conserve water resources in rural areas and enhance groundwater levels

title: Cut foreign travel, use more EVs: Finance Ministry’s ‘austerity’ steps for public sector banks & insurers
content: Circular does not mention the West Asia crisis, but it comes a little more than a week after Prime Minister Narendra Modi exhorted the nation to adopt similar austerity measures.

title: Palghar tempo-truck collision: 13 people die; CM Fadnavis announces compensation
content: An tempo packed with more than 50 passengers heading for an engagement ceremony collided with a speeding container truck near Dhaniwari village

title: Modi in Norway highlights: PM holds interaction with Norwegian business leaders
content: PM Modi was conferred with Norway's highest civilian honour, the Grand Cross of the Royal Norwegian Order of Merit

title: Probe Panel Submits Reports To Lok Sabha Speaker In Judge Cash Recovery Case
content: In August last year, Lok Sabha Speaker Birla then constituted a three-member Judges Inquiry Committee to look into the charges.

title: Medical Student Stabbed To Death In Punjab: Cops
content: Preliminary investigations revealed that the victim was stabbed three to four times in the neck, leading to his death on the spot.

title: 3 Killed In Shooting At US' San Diego Mosque, 2 Suspects Dead: Cops
content: The center is the largest mosque in San Diego County and includes the Al Rashid School that offers courses in Arabic language, Islamic studies and the Quran for students ages 5 and up.

title: Cuba Warns Of "Bloodbath" If US Attacks
content: Cuba's leader warned Monday of a "bloodbath" in the event of an American attack, while the US Treasury sanctioned Cuba's main intelligence agency and top leaders as tensions spiked between the arch-foes.

title: Meta Plans May 20 Layoffs, To Cut 10% Of Its Global Workforce
content: As part of that, the company plans to move 7,000 employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows and to eliminate managerial roles, she said.

title: Key Moments In Elon Musk Vs OpenAI Trial
content: The jury decided Musk waited too long to sue. Musk had sought about $150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft, a major investor, to be paid to OpenAI's nonprofit. Musk also sought the removal of Altman and Brockman.

title: Trump Holds Off On Planned Iran Strike After Gulf Allies' Request
content: Trump's announcement in a social media post Monday came as he had threated the clock was ticking for Iran to strike a deal or fighting would renew after a fragile ceasefire.

title: Manipur Tense As Blockades By Kuki, Naga Groups Paralyse National Highway
content: The parallel blockades have further intensified the crisis in the conflict-hit state, with transport services, essential supplies and movement of goods along NH-2 facing major disruption.

title: US Iran War News Live Updates: Trump Holds Off Iran Attack Planned For Tuesday After Gulf Intervention
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: Centre Tightens Jal Jeevan Spending Rules To Curb Inflated Costs, Tender Irregularities
content: According to Central government sources, the aim is to reduce costs and put an end to irregularities.

title: 'Sidelined' Senior Cop Gets Key Role Under Suvendu Adhikari Government
content: Damayanti Sen, who was leading the Park Street rape case probe, will now act as the member secretary of a committee led by retired Justice Samapti Chatterjee.

title: Bengaluru Left Waterlogged, Traffic Hit After Heavy Rain
content: The heavy downpour also led to multiple incidents of trees and branches falling in different parts of Bengaluru, posing inconvenience to commuters and motorists.

title: 'Nuclear Plants Must Not Be Targets': US Expert To NDTV After Strike In UAE
content: The Barakah incident comes at a time when nuclear infrastructure is increasingly finding itself near active conflict zones.

title: Assam Crime Branch Summons Randeep Surjewala In Pawan Khera Case Probe
content: According to sources, the Crime Branch of Assam Police has directed Surjewala to appear before investigators in Guwahati on May 23.

title: Team Trump Creates $1.7 Billion Fund For Allies Prosecuted Under Biden
content: US President Donald Trump's Justice Department announced on Monday the creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate political allies prosecuted under the Biden administration.

title: 60-Page PDF That Alerted NEET Paper Leak Whistleblowers Accessed By NDTV
content: The 60-page document contained the questions and their corresponding answers listed in sequential order. This was the file on basis of which the paper leak was established and exposed.

title: UK Cops Find 90-kg Cocaine In Shipment Of Kim Kardashian's Shapewear Brand
content: A British court sentenced a truck driver to prison for smuggling $9.4 million worth of cocaine in the shipment.

title: Govt assigns ARAI to carry out study on impact of E25 fuel on existing vehicles
content: ARAI tasked country's premier vehicle testing agency to carry out a detailed study on the impact of E25 fuel (25% ethanol blended petrol) on different performance aspects, particularly mileage and engine condition, of existing vehicles . move comes as government accelerates plan to increase blending in bid to reduce crude imports .

title: FIR filed against 2 Punjab cops for making dead man ‘give statement’
content: Payal Dhawan is a Principal Correspondent, with the Times of India in Ludhiana. She covers crime, prisons, police administration, vigilance bureau, forest & wildlife department and Congress party. Associated with the organisation since 2013, earlier she covered sports and education. Read More

title: 'Illegal' notice pasted on Abhishek's properties
content: the notice is KMC's first step when it starts a probe into alleged irregularities in a property . officials pasted notices under Section 401 of the Kolkata Municipal Corporation Act, 1980 . the notice was issued when at least a part of a building is constructed without the civic body's approval and paves the way for demolition .

title: Anti-Naxal security camps turn 'seva deras' in Bastar
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: Sqn Ldr Saanya makes history, becomes IAF's first woman Cat-A qualified flying instructor
content: Squadron leader Saanya became the first woman officer to earn the coveted Cat-A Qualified Flying Instructor (QFI) qualification . the air force said, 'Excellence takes flight. her achievement embodies dedication and relentless pursuit of excellence'

title: Haryana land deal: Vadra withdraws plea, will appear before trial court
content: ED had earlier countered the plea saying it lacked merit . he had questioned the merit of ED's case related to a land deal in shikohpur . but the offences forming the basis of the case were only included in 2013 . now that he has withdrawn the challenge, he will have to appear before the trial court to participate in proceedings on the charges to be framed against him .

content: ex-chhattisgarh bureaucrat anil Tuteja will come out of jail more than two years after his arrest in April 2024 . he was granted bail in the DMF scam case on monday . but he had been in custody since his first arrest on April 21, 2024, advocate says .

title: Indian restaurant owner living in Japan for 30 years breaks down in parliament
content: manish Kumar, an Indian-origin restaurateur, has been living in the country for the past 30 years . he broke down during an event in the japanese parliament while sharing his story . his business visa has been denied and he is forced to shut doha .

title: NEET leak: CBI arrests coaching centre founder
content: Shivraj Raghunath Motegaonkar allegedly circulated leaked NEETUG 2026 questions through coaching networks and “mock tests” conducted before examination . this is the 10th arrest by CBI, which has now widened its probe, bringing more than 60 suspected beneficiaries under scrutiny .

title: Judicial panel submits report on judge Varma
content: three-member committee looking into allegations of corruption against Yashwant Varma submitted its report to LS Speaker Om Birla . the report will be laid in Parliament in due course under the Judges (Inquiry) Act .

title: SC bench slams own court ruling denying bail to Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam
content: a bench of justices said it was 'difficult to follow' the division-bench judgment in Delhi riots case . it contradicted the 2021 three-judge Najeeb case verdict . 'bail is the rule, jail the exception' wasn’t just a slogan flowing from CrPC .

title: US extends waiver on Russian crude imports by 1 month
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: Ground staff’s 3-hr flash strike leaves AI passengers stranded at Mumbai airport
content: air India air services Ltd ground handling staff stage impromptu demonstration . 31 departures were delayed, 5 arrival flights saw delayed parking . baggage delivery for 6 international and 33 domestic flights was delayed . the disruption affected check-in, baggage handling and cargo loading and unloading operations .

title: From Arctic to outer space: India, Norway clinch deals
content: the countries upgraded ties to a green strategic partnership, signed deals for cooperation in outer space, health and technology . both sides concluded a triangular development cooperation agreement that will allow them to jointly contribute to human development in countries of the global South . india, Norway condemn terror, call for early end to conflicts .

title: Haryana native, 23, becomes UK's youngest Indian-origin mayor
content: Tushar Kumar, who lived in Haryana's Rohtak until he was 10, was elected mayor of Elstree and Borehamwood Town Council last week . his parents, who work in education, had moved to the uk from the country 13 years ago . he has a civic role and will be attending events .
